input.content: You are a helpful assistant with workspace tools. Use the tools as instructed.

content.text: Use the list_files tool to list files. Use defaults.

tools.name: mastra_workspace_read_file
tools.description: Read a file from the workspace filesystem. Text files come back as text — use offset/limit to read a line range from large files. Supported media files come back as a native file part you can view directly. Other binary files return only their metadata (path, size, mime type) since their raw contents are not useful to read.

path.description: The path to the file to read (e.g., "data/config.json")

offset.description:
Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to line 1 if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

anyOf.description:
Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to line 1 if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

limit.description:
Maximum number of lines to read. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to the end of the file if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

anyOf.description:
Maximum number of lines to read. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to the end of the file if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

showLineNumbers.description: Prefix each line with its line number. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to true if omitted.

anyOf.description: Prefix each line with its line number. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to true if omitted.

encoding.description: Usually omit this — text files and supported media are handled automatically. Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

anyOf.description: Usually omit this — text files and supported media are handled automatically. Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

tools.name: mastra_workspace_write_file
tools.description: Write content to a file in the workspace filesystem. Creates parent directories if needed.

path.description: The path where to write the file (e.g., "data/output.txt")

content.description: The content to write to the file

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists

anyOf.description: Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists

tools.name: mastra_workspace_edit_file
tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

path.description: The path to the file to edit

old_string.description: The exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file.

new_string.description: The text to replace old_string with

replace_all.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

anyOf.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

tools.name: mastra_workspace_list_files

path.description: Directory path to list

anyOf.description: Directory path to list

maxDepth.description: Maximum depth to descend (default: 2). Similar to tree -L flag.

anyOf.description: Maximum depth to descend (default: 2). Similar to tree -L flag.

showHidden.description: Show hidden files starting with "." (default: false). Similar to tree -a flag.

anyOf.description: Show hidden files starting with "." (default: false). Similar to tree -a flag.

dirsOnly.description: List directories only, no files (default: false). Similar to tree -d flag.

anyOf.description: List directories only, no files (default: false). Similar to tree -d flag.

exclude.description: Pattern to exclude (e.g., "node_modules"). Similar to tree -I flag.

anyOf.description: Pattern to exclude (e.g., "node_modules"). Similar to tree -I flag.

extension.description: Filter by file extension (e.g., ".ts"). Similar to tree -P flag.

anyOf.description: Filter by file extension (e.g., ".ts"). Similar to tree -P flag.

pattern.description: Glob pattern(s) to filter files. Omit this parameter to list all files (do NOT pass "*"). Use "**/*.ext" to match files recursively across directories. "*" only matches within a single directory level (standard glob). Glob patterns only filter files — directories are always shown to preserve tree structure. Examples: "**/*.ts", "src/**/*.test.ts", "*.config.{js,ts}".

respectGitignore.description: Respect .gitignore in the listed directory (default: true).

anyOf.description: Respect .gitignore in the listed directory (default: true).

tools.name: mastra_workspace_delete
tools.description: Delete a file or directory from the workspace filesystem

path.description: The path to the file or directory to delete

recursive.description: If true, delete directories and their contents recursively. Required for non-empty directories.

anyOf.description: If true, delete directories and their contents recursively. Required for non-empty directories.

tools.description: Get file or directory metadata from the workspace. Returns existence, type, size, and modification time.

path.description: The path to check

tools.description: Create a directory in the workspace filesystem

path.description: The path of the directory to create

pattern.description: Regex pattern to search for

anyOf.description: File, directory, or glob pattern to search within (default: "."). A plain path searches that file or directory. A glob pattern (e.g., "**/*.ts", "src/**/*.test.ts") filters which files to search.

contextLines.description: Number of lines of context to include before and after each match (default: 0)

anyOf.description: Number of lines of context to include before and after each match (default: 0)

anyOf.description: Maximum matches per file. Moves on to the next file after this many matches. Similar to grep -m flag.

Structured transformation to apply

anyOf.description: Required for remove-import and rename transforms. The current name to target.

newName.description: Required for rename transform. The new name to replace targetName with.

tools.name: mastra_workspace_lsp_inspect
tools.description: Inspect code at a specific symbol position using the Language Server Protocol. Provide an absolute file path, a 1-indexed line number, and the exact line content with <<< marking the cursor position. Exactly one <<< marker is required. Returns hover information, any diagnostics reported on that line, plus definition and implementation locations when available. Use this for type information, symbol navigation, and go-to-definition; use view to read the surrounding implementation.

path.description: Absolute path to the file

match.description: Line content with <<< marking the cursor position. Exactly one <<< marker is required. Example: "const foo = <<<bar()" means cursor is at bar

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.name: mastra_workspace_read_file
tools.description: Read a file from the workspace filesystem. Text files come back as text — use offset/limit to read a line range from large files. Supported media files come back as a native file part you can view directly. Other binary files return only their metadata (path, size, mime type) since their raw contents are not useful to read.

path.description: The path to the file to read (e.g., "data/config.json")

offset.description:
Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to line 1 if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

anyOf.description:
Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to line 1 if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

limit.description:
Maximum number of lines to read. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to the end of the file if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

anyOf.description:
Maximum number of lines to read. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to the end of the file if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

showLineNumbers.description: Prefix each line with its line number. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to true if omitted.

anyOf.description: Prefix each line with its line number. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to true if omitted.

encoding.description: Usually omit this — text files and supported media are handled automatically. Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

anyOf.description: Usually omit this — text files and supported media are handled automatically. Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

tools.name: mastra_workspace_edit_file
tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

path.description: The path to the file to edit

old_string.description: The exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file.

new_string.description: The text to replace old_string with

replace_all.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

anyOf.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

anyOf.description: Maximum depth to descend (default: 2). Similar to tree -L flag.

showHidden.description: Show hidden files starting with "." (default: false). Similar to tree -a flag.

anyOf.description: Show hidden files starting with "." (default: false). Similar to tree -a flag.

dirsOnly.description: List directories only, no files (default: false). Similar to tree -d flag.

anyOf.description: List directories only, no files (default: false). Similar to tree -d flag.

exclude.description: Pattern to exclude (e.g., "node_modules"). Similar to tree -I flag.

anyOf.description: Pattern to exclude (e.g., "node_modules"). Similar to tree -I flag.

extension.description: Filter by file extension (e.g., ".ts"). Similar to tree -P flag.

anyOf.description: Filter by file extension (e.g., ".ts"). Similar to tree -P flag.

pattern.description: Glob pattern(s) to filter files. Omit this parameter to list all files (do NOT pass "*"). Use "**/*.ext" to match files recursively across directories. "*" only matches within a single directory level (standard glob). Glob patterns only filter files — directories are always shown to preserve tree structure. Examples: "**/*.ts", "src/**/*.test.ts", "*.config.{js,ts}".

respectGitignore.description: Respect .gitignore in the listed directory (default: true).

anyOf.description: Respect .gitignore in the listed directory (default: true).

- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.name: mastra_workspace_edit_file
tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.name: mastra_workspace_lsp_inspect
tools.description: Inspect code at a specific symbol position using the Language Server Protocol. Provide an absolute file path, a 1-indexed line number, and the exact line content with <<< marking the cursor position. Exactly one <<< marker is required. Returns hover information, any diagnostics reported on that line, plus definition and implementation locations when available. Use this for type information, symbol navigation, and go-to-definition; use view to read the surrounding implementation.

path.description: Absolute path to the file

match.description: Line content with <<< marking the cursor position. Exactly one <<< marker is required. Example: "const foo = <<<bar()" means cursor is at bar

summary.description: A brief summary of what was done

tools.description: Read a file from the workspace filesystem. Text files come back as text — use offset/limit to read a line range from large files. Supported media files come back as a native file part you can view directly. Other binary files return only their metadata (path, size, mime type) since their raw contents are not useful to read.
tools.name: mastra_workspace_read_file

path.description: The path to the file to read (e.g., "data/config.json")

offset.description:
Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to line 1 if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

anyOf.description:
Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to line 1 if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

limit.description:
Maximum number of lines to read. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to the end of the file if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

anyOf.description:
Maximum number of lines to read. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to the end of the file if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

showLineNumbers.description: Prefix each line with its line number. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to true if omitted.

anyOf.description: Prefix each line with its line number. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to true if omitted.

encoding.description: Usually omit this — text files and supported media are handled automatically. Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

anyOf.description: Usually omit this — text files and supported media are handled automatically. Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

path.description: The path where to write the file (e.g., "data/output.txt")

content.description: The content to write to the file

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists

anyOf.description: Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.
tools.name: mastra_workspace_edit_file

path.description: The path to the file to edit

old_string.description: The exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file.

new_string.description: The text to replace old_string with

replace_all.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

anyOf.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

anyOf.description: Directory path to list

showHidden.description: Show hidden files starting with "." (default: false). Similar to tree -a flag.

anyOf.description: Show hidden files starting with "." (default: false). Similar to tree -a flag.

dirsOnly.description: List directories only, no files (default: false). Similar to tree -d flag.

anyOf.description: List directories only, no files (default: false). Similar to tree -d flag.

anyOf.description: Pattern to exclude (e.g., "node_modules"). Similar to tree -I flag.

extension.description: Filter by file extension (e.g., ".ts"). Similar to tree -P flag.

anyOf.description: Filter by file extension (e.g., ".ts"). Similar to tree -P flag.

pattern.description: Glob pattern(s) to filter files. Omit this parameter to list all files (do NOT pass "*"). Use "**/*.ext" to match files recursively across directories. "*" only matches within a single directory level (standard glob). Glob patterns only filter files — directories are always shown to preserve tree structure. Examples: "**/*.ts", "src/**/*.test.ts", "*.config.{js,ts}".

respectGitignore.description: Respect .gitignore in the listed directory (default: true).

anyOf.description: Respect .gitignore in the listed directory (default: true).

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

old_string.description: The exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file.

anyOf.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

tools.name: mastra_workspace_edit_file
tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

old_string.description: The exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file.

anyOf.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

tools.name: mastra_workspace_lsp_inspect
tools.description: Inspect code at a specific symbol position using the Language Server Protocol. Provide an absolute file path, a 1-indexed line number, and the exact line content with <<< marking the cursor position. Exactly one <<< marker is required. Returns hover information, any diagnostics reported on that line, plus definition and implementation locations when available. Use this for type information, symbol navigation, and go-to-definition; use view to read the surrounding implementation.

path.description: Absolute path to the file

match.description: Line content with <<< marking the cursor position. Exactly one <<< marker is required. Example: "const foo = <<<bar()" means cursor is at bar

tools.description: Read a file from the workspace filesystem. Text files come back as text — use offset/limit to read a line range from large files. Supported media files come back as a native file part you can view directly. Other binary files return only their metadata (path, size, mime type) since their raw contents are not useful to read.
tools.name: mastra_workspace_read_file

path.description: The path to the file to read (e.g., "data/config.json")

offset.description:
Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to line 1 if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

anyOf.description:
Line number to start reading from (1-indexed). Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to line 1 if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

limit.description:
Maximum number of lines to read. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to the end of the file if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

anyOf.description:
Maximum number of lines to read. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to the end of the file if omitted.
constraints: greater than or equal to 1

showLineNumbers.description: Prefix each line with its line number. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to true if omitted.

anyOf.description: Prefix each line with its line number. Only used when reading text files; ignored for media and other binary files. Defaults to true if omitted.

encoding.description: Usually omit this — text files and supported media are handled automatically. Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

anyOf.description: Usually omit this — text files and supported media are handled automatically. Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.
tools.name: mastra_workspace_edit_file

path.description: The path to the file to edit

old_string.description: The exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file.

new_string.description: The text to replace old_string with

replace_all.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

anyOf.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

pattern.description: Glob pattern(s) to filter files. Omit this parameter to list all files (do NOT pass "*"). Use "**/*.ext" to match files recursively across directories. "*" only matches within a single directory level (standard glob). Glob patterns only filter files — directories are always shown to preserve tree structure. Examples: "**/*.ts", "src/**/*.test.ts", "*.config.{js,ts}".

tools.name: mastra_workspace_edit_file
tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.

- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

anyOf.description: Usually omit this — text files and supported media are handled automatically. Pass `base64` or `hex` to get the file's raw bytes encoded as text when you need to inspect an unsupported binary file that would otherwise only return metadata (e.g. checking a file header or magic bytes).

tools.name: mastra_workspace_edit_file
tools.description:
Edit a file by replacing specific text. The old_string must match exactly and be unique in the file.

Usage:
- Read the file first to get the exact text to replace.
- By default, read file output includes line number prefixes (e.g., "     1→"). Ensure you preserve the exact indentation as it appears AFTER the arrow. Never include any part of the line number prefix in old_string or new_string.
- Include enough surrounding context (multiple lines) to make old_string unique. If it still isn't unique, include more lines.
- Use replace_all only when intentionally replacing all occurrences.

path.description: The path to the file to edit

old_string.description: The exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file.

new_string.description: The text to replace old_string with

replace_all.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

anyOf.description: If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), old_string must be unique.

tools.name: mastra_workspace_lsp_inspect
tools.description: Inspect code at a specific symbol position using the Language Server Protocol. Provide an absolute file path, a 1-indexed line number, and the exact line content with <<< marking the cursor position. Exactly one <<< marker is required. Returns hover information, any diagnostics reported on that line, plus definition and implementation locations when available. Use this for type information, symbol navigation, and go-to-definition; use view to read the surrounding implementation.

path.description: Absolute path to the file

match.description: Line content with <<< marking the cursor position. Exactly one <<< marker is required. Example: "const foo = <<<bar()" means cursor is at bar

content.text: {"summary":"I found a directory and two files in the workspace.","filesFound":2,"details":"The directory is named `subdir`, and it contains a file `nested.ts`. At the root level, there's also a file named `hello.txt`."}

summary.description: A brief summary of what was done

tools.description: Read a file from the workspace filesystem. Text files come back as text — use offset/limit to read a line range from large files. Supported media files come back as a native file part you can view directly. Other binary files return only their metadata (path, size, mime type) since their raw contents are not useful to read.

constraints: greater than or equal to 1

anyOf.description:

constraints: greater than or equal to 1

limit.description:

constraints: greater than or equal to 1
